package_name: Will Kelly custom GPT content pipeline
positioning: An interview-ready example of a governed AI content pipeline for technical and marketing content.
use_case: Show how AI can operationalize content strategy, technical accuracy, marketing alignment, review discipline, and repurposing without reducing content work to generic drafting.
pipeline_principle: AI drafts are pre-review artifacts. The system is designed to produce structured work, identify uncertainty, force verification, and preserve narrative consistency across technical and marketing assets.

pipeline_stages.purpose: Translate vague requests into structured briefs tied to audience, business problem, technical truth, proof, constraints, and review path.
Select the right workflow, assign asset type, enforce handoff contracts, and prevent skipped review gates.
Draft explainers, docs, tutorials, solution briefs, and technical enablement artifacts with assumptions and verification flags.
Draft messaging, thought leadership, web copy, campaign content, and sales enablement material grounded in pain points and proof.
Score clarity, claims, technical accuracy, buyer relevance, consistency, and publication readiness.
Turn approved core assets into channel-specific derivatives while preserving claims, caveats, and narrative spine.

interview_angle: This is not a prompt library. It is a content operating model encoded as reusable GPT behavior.
It shows how technical content, marketing content, and enablement can share one source of truth without becoming bland consensus sludge.
It creates measurable gates: intake completeness, claim risk, SME review status, publication readiness, and reuse potential.
It demonstrates practical AI enablement: workflows, standards, handoffs, and verification rather than one-off drafting tricks.
